mission.title: Install and Configure Kubefleet on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeFleet is an open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster application management solution that simplifies governance and workload management across multiple clusters. Installing KubeFleet allows you to efficiently manage applications in a hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

steps.title: Create the KubeFleet Namespace
steps.description: First, create a namespace for KubeFleet to operate within. This helps in organizing resources and managing permissions effectively.

steps.title: Apply KubeFleet Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the necessary manifests from the KubeFleet repository. You can use the following commands to deploy the components.

steps.title: Verify KubeFleet Installation
steps.description: To ensure that KubeFleet has been installed correctly, check the status of the pods in the kubefleet-system namespace.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeFleet will show the hub agent pod running in the 'fleet-system' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for the hub agent.

uninstall.title: Remove KubeFleet Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall KubeFleet, delete the resources you applied earlier. Ensure you are in the correct namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeFleet
upgrade.description: To upgrade KubeFleet, apply the new version of the manifests. Replace 'v0.1.0' with the desired version.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your cluster.
